children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 1 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary, acting through the Director of the United States Geological Survey.

children.heading: Secretary of Commerce
children.label: 2 Secretary of Commerce
children.text: The term “Secretary of Commerce” means the Secretary of Commerce, acting through the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere.

children.heading: System
children.label: 3 System
children.text: The term “System” means the National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System established under subsection (b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Volcanic Ash Advisory Center
children.label: 4 Volcanic Ash Advisory Center
children.text: The term “Volcanic Ash Advisory Center” means an entity designated by the International Civil Aviation Organization that is responsible for informing aviation interests about the presence of volcanic ash in the airspace.

children.heading: National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System
children.label: b National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the United States Geological Survey a system, to be known as the “National Volcano Early Warning and Monitoring System”, to monitor, warn, and protect citizens of the United States from undue and avoidable harm from volcanic activity.

children.label: i
children.text: to organize, modernize, standardize, and stabilize the monitoring systems of the volcano observatories in the United States, which includes the Alaska Volcano Observatory, California Volcano Observatory, Cascades Volcano Observatory, Hawaiian Volcano Observatory, and Yellowstone Volcano Observatory;

children.label: ii
children.text: to unify the monitoring systems of volcano observatories in the United States into a single interoperative system; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to strengthen the warning and monitoring systems of volcano observatories in the United States by integrating relevant capacities of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, including with the Volcanic Ash Advisory Centers located in Anchorage, Alaska, and Washington, D.C., to observe and model emissions of gases, aerosols, and ash, atmospheric dynamics and chemistry, and ocean chemistry resulting from volcanic eruptions.

children.label: i
children.text: upgrading existing networks on monitored volcanoes;

children.label: ii
children.text: installing new networks on unmonitored volcanoes; and

children.label: iii
children.text: employing geodetic and other components when applicable.

children.label: i
children.text: a national volcano watch office that is operational 24 hours a day and 7 days a week;

children.label: ii
children.text: a national volcano data center; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an external grants program to support research in volcano monitoring science and technology.

children.heading: Modernization activities
children.label: B Modernization activities
children.text: Modernization activities under the System shall include the comprehensive application of emerging technologies, including digital broadband seismometers, real-time continuous Global Positioning System receivers, satellite and airborne radar interferometry, acoustic pressure sensors, spectrometry to measure gas emissions, and unoccupied aerial vehicles.

children.heading: Memorandum of understanding
children.label: C Memorandum of understanding
children.text: The Secretary and the Secretary of Commerce shall develop and execute a memorandum of understanding to establish cooperative support for the activities of the System from the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, including environmental observations, modeling, and temporary duty assignments of personnel to support emergency activities, as necessary or appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a 5-year management plan for establishing and operating the System.March 12, 2019

children.label: I
children.text: annual cost estimates for modernization activities and operation of the System;

children.label: II
children.text: annual milestones, standards, and performance goals; and

children.label: III
children.text: recommendations for, and progress towards, establishing new, or enhancing existing, partnerships to leverage resources.

children.heading: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration cost estimates
children.label: I National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration cost estimates
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce shall submit to the Secretary annual cost estimates for modernization activities and support of the System for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.

children.heading: Update of management plan
children.label: II Update of management plan
children.text: The Secretary shall update the management plan submitted under clause (i) to include the cost estimates submitted under subclause (I).

children.heading: Advisory committee
children.label: B Advisory committee
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an advisory committee to assist the Secretary in implementing the System, to be comprised of representatives of relevant agencies and members of the scientific community, to be appointed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: C Partnerships
children.text: The Secretary may enter into cooperative agreements with institutions of higher education and State agencies designating the institutions of higher education and State agencies as volcano observatory partners for the System.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of Transportation;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency.

children.heading: Collaboration
children.label: E Collaboration
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce shall collaborate with the Secretary to implement activities carried out under this section related to the expertise of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, including observations and modeling of emissions of gases, aerosols, and ash, atmospheric dynamics and chemistry, and ocean chemistry resulting from volcanic eruptions.

children.heading: Annual report
children.label: 4 Annual report
children.text: Annually, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report that describes the activities carried out under this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations, United States Geological Survey
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations, United States Geological Survey
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the United States Geological Survey to carry out this section $55,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for the period of fiscal years 2023 through 2024.

children.heading: Effect on other sources of Federal funding
children.label: 3 Effect on other sources of Federal funding
children.text: Amounts made available under this subsection shall supplement, and not supplant, Federal funds made available for other hazards activities and programs of the United States Geological Survey and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration.
